message:
refactor(ffs-extent): switch test MemBlockDevice to parking_lot::Mutex

The ffs-extent crate already pulls parking_lot::RwLock into the
non-test code. Having the test MemBlockDevice continue to use
std::sync::Mutex forced every read_block / write_block in the test
module to call `.lock().unwrap()` on a Result<MutexGuard, PoisonError>
that nothing in the test path can actually poison — the mutex is
local to a single test's block device and is never held across a
panic-producing assertion. parking_lot::Mutex has no poison state and
returns the guard directly, so the `.unwrap()` calls disappear.

Changes:

- Test imports: `use std::sync::Mutex;` -> `use parking_lot::Mutex;`.
- MemBlockDevice::read_block: `self.blocks.lock().unwrap()` becomes
  `self.blocks.lock()`.
- MemBlockDevice::write_block: same cleanup on the insert path.
- Remove the now-unused
  `#[expect(clippy::match_wildcard_for_single_variants)]` attribute
  on the tests module — d571577 migrated the remaining wildcard match
  arms into `found_extent` helper calls, so nothing inside the module
  still triggers the lint.

Behaviourally identical; just removes the spurious Result handling
and drops a dead lint escape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
